findings.rule: All column IDs split with .rsplit('.', 1), never .split('.', 1)
findings.details: Checked all new and changed code in backend/parsers/sql.py and backend/lineage/engine.py. Every split of a column/table identifier uses .rsplit('.', 1) or .rsplit('.', 1)[0]/[1]. No .split('.', 1) call found in changed code. Grep across backend/ confirmed zero violations.

The new _parse_merge function delegates expression stringification to _classify_transform (which calls node.sql(dialect='databricks')) and to on_expr.sql(dialect='databricks') in _parse_select_node. All new SQL expression renders in the changed file use dialect='databricks'. sqlglot.parse / sqlglot.parse_one calls at lines 920 and 846 both pass dialect='databricks'.

findings.rule: Frontend API additions must go in lib/api.ts as typed fetch wrappers
findings.details: New SourceFile type and api.sources.files() wrapper correctly added to lib/api.ts following the existing apiFetch<T> pattern. No backend URLs hardcoded; all calls route through the BASE = '/api/backend' proxy.

findings.rule: Frontend data fetching must use React Query hooks in lib/hooks.ts
findings.details: New useSourceFiles hook added to lib/hooks.ts using useQuery with queryKey, queryFn, and enabled guard. Pattern matches all existing hooks (useSources, useColumns, etc.). Mutations correctly invalidate relevant query keys on success.

findings.rule: Never use NEXT_PUBLIC_ for the backend URL
findings.details: Grep across all frontend changed files found zero occurrences of NEXT_PUBLIC_. The new api.sources.files call routes through the existing BASE = '/api/backend' constant which resolves server-side through the Next.js API proxy.

findings.rule: Frontend components must use id.lastIndexOf('.') to split table from column
findings.details: lineage-graph.tsx (changed file) defines splitColId() using id.lastIndexOf('.') at line 31, which is correct for 4-part catalog.schema.table.column IDs. warnings-panel.tsx (new file) does not perform any column ID splitting — not applicable. sources/page.tsx uses f.file.split('/') to extract a filename path segment, which is a file system path split, not a column ID split — correct.

residual_risks.description: Pre-existing bug in _resolve_temp_views (sql.py line 739): the guard 'src_tbl not in temp_views' compares a lowercased src_tbl against the original (potentially mixed-case) temp_views set rather than temp_views_lower. This is unchanged pre-existing code, out of scope for this review, but could silently skip lookup for mixed-case temp view names in chains.

The /sources/{source_id}/files endpoint does not invalidate or distinguish between stale stats from a previous refresh cycle and a fresh one. If a source is re-refreshed, old file_stats are replaced (correct), but the endpoint returns an empty list before first refresh rather than surfacing a 'not yet parsed' state to the client. The frontend handles this with 'No parsed files. Refresh the source to parse.' — acceptable but not explicitly tested.

testing_gaps.description: No test covers the /sources/{source_id}/files endpoint. The confidence tier logic (high/medium/low) based on approximate_count, warning_count, and error_files is untested.
testing_gaps.recommended_test: test_routes.py: register + refresh a source with known approximate and certain edges, call GET /sources/{id}/files, assert confidence values are correct for each tier.

testing_gaps.description: No test covers MERGE INTO statement parsing in _parse_merge. The WHEN MATCHED THEN UPDATE and WHEN NOT MATCHED THEN INSERT branches are both new code paths with no test coverage.
testing_gaps.recommended_test: test_sql_parser.py: add cases for MERGE INTO with aliased source/target, UPDATE SET, and INSERT VALUES branches; assert correct source_col/target_col in returned edges.

overall: PASS — no CLAUDE.md rule violations found in changed code. Two testing gaps noted for newly introduced logic paths.
